question: What is Ayonic?
answer: Ayonic is an online platform that connects customers needing tasks or services with local service providers who can fulfill those tasks. You can use it to book help for cleaning, handyman work, moving, or other home and local services.

question: How do I book a service on Ayonic?
answer: Browse available service categories, choose the type of service you want, fill in the details such as location, date/time, and special instructions, and submit a booking request. Then a provider accepts it or you may select from available ones.

question: Do I need an account to use Ayonic?
answer: Yes — you need to create an account to book services, manage your bookings, and rate providers.

question: Can I book multiple services at once?
answer: Usually yes — you can schedule more than one service for different times or even at the same time if providers are available.

question: How do you verify service providers?
answer: Providers go through a verification process which may include identity checks, background checks, proof of qualifications, licensing where applicable, and verifying references or past work experience.

question: Can I choose my preferred provider?
answer: Yes — once providers are shown or matched, you can often choose from among available providers, or rebook someone you have used before if they accept.

question: What if the service provider does not show up?
answer: If a provider fails to show up, you can report it through the platform. Usually there is a resolution process including a refund, rescheduling, or assigning another provider.

question: Can I rate or review a provider after the service?
answer: Yes — customers can leave ratings and reviews for providers to help future customers make decisions and to maintain service quality on the platform.

question: What payment methods are accepted on Ayonic?
answer: Accepted payment methods include credit and debit cards, PayPal, RazorPay, Paystack, Stripe, Flutterwave, and possibly bank transfers.

question: When will I be charged for a booking?
answer: Usually at the time of booking or once the provider accepts the job. Some bookings may hold the funds until after the service is delivered.

question: Are payments secure on Ayonic?
answer: Yes — the platform uses encrypted payment processing and secure payment gateways to protect your financial data. Ayonic does not store full credit card numbers.

question: Can I pay the provider directly in cash?
answer: No — all payments must go through the platform so that there is accountability, proper records, and protection for refunds and disputes.

question: Can I cancel a booking?
answer: Yes, bookings can be cancelled according to the platform's cancellation policy.

question: What is the cancellation policy?
answer: The policy depends on timing. A full refund may apply if cancelled more than 24 hours before the service. A partial refund or no refund may apply if cancelled close to the service time.

question: How do I reschedule a service?
answer: Contact support or use the platform to change the booking details such as date, time, or provider, subject to availability.

question: Will I get a refund if I cancel?
answer: Possibly — it depends on when you cancel and the terms. Cancelling too close to the service time may result in a cancellation fee or reduced refund.

question: How does Ayonic ensure customer safety?
answer: Through provider background verification, insurance requirements, identity checks, platform guarantees or insurance for services, and strict adherence to safety standards.

question: What should I do if I have an issue with a provider?
answer: Contact customer support via the platform, submit a complaint through the resolution process, provide evidence such as photos or communication records, and the platform will mediate the dispute.

question: Is my personal data protected on Ayonic?
answer: Yes — the platform uses secure systems, data encryption, and privacy policies. It stores only the minimum required data and follows data protection laws including GDPR.

question: How can I contact Ayonic customer support?
answer: You can reach us via email at support@ayonic.com, by phone, or through a support form in the app or website. There is also a Help or Contact Us section available on the platform.

question: What are Ayonic's support hours?
answer: Typically business hours in the local timezone. For urgent issues you can email support@ayonic.com at any time.

question: What personal data does Ayonic collect from all users?
answer: From all users we collect account information including first and last name, email address, phone number and password, communication data from in-app chat and emails, and technical data such as IP address, device type, operating system, and app usage statistics.

question: What extra data does Ayonic collect from clients?
answer: From clients we additionally collect the physical address where the task will be performed, and payment information such as credit card details which are processed securely by a third-party provider. Ayonic does not store full card numbers.

question: What extra data does Ayonic collect from providers?
answer: From providers we additionally collect verification data including a copy of your ID card, Gewerbeschein (Trade License), and tax identification numbers, payout information including IBAN/BIC bank details, and profile data including your photo, skills, hourly rates, and client reviews.

question: Why does Ayonic process my personal data?
answer: We process your data to provide the service under Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR, to comply with German commercial and tax law under Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR, for legitimate interests such as fraud prevention and security under Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR, and with your consent for marketing emails and non-essential cookies under Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.

question: How long does Ayonic keep my account data?
answer: Account and profile data is kept for the duration of your active account. After account deletion it is deleted within 30 days unless statutory retention obligations apply.

question: How long does Ayonic keep invoices and payment records?
answer: Invoices and transaction records are kept for 6 to 10 years as required by German commercial and tax law (HGB and AO).

question: How long does Ayonic keep support inquiry data?
answer: Support inquiry data is kept for up to 3 years after the last interaction to defend against potential legal claims.

question: How long does Ayonic keep technical log data?
answer: Technical and security log data including IP addresses is stored for up to 30 days and then automatically deleted or anonymized, unless needed longer for security incident investigation.

question: How long does Ayonic keep analytics data?
answer: Analytics and usage data is stored in pseudonymized or aggregated form for up to 24 months, unless users withdraw consent earlier.

question: How long does Ayonic keep marketing data?
answer: Marketing and consent-based data is stored until consent is withdrawn or the purpose no longer applies, after which it is deleted without undue delay.

question: What are my GDPR rights with Ayonic?
answer: Under GDPR you have the right of access to your data, right to rectification of inaccurate data, right to erasure (right to be forgotten), right to restrict processing, right to data portability, and right to object to processing. Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw it at any time.

question: How do I exercise my GDPR rights with Ayonic?
answer: Email support@ayonic.com to request access, correction, deletion, restriction, or portability of your data. You can also withdraw consent for marketing or cookies at any time through the consent management settings.

question: Who is the data controller for Ayonic?
answer: Ayonic (Kyaani GmbH), Leipziger Platz 15, 10117 Berlin, Germany. Contact email: support@ayonic.com.

question: Who can I complain to about Ayonic's data practices?
answer: You can lodge a complaint with the Berlin Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (Berliner Beauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit), or with the data protection authority in your country of residence.

question: Does Ayonic share my data with other users?
answer: Yes, limited data is shared as necessary. When a client books a task, the client's first name and task address are shared with the confirmed provider. The provider's profile, photo, and reviews are shared with the client.

question: Does Ayonic share my data with third party companies?
answer: Yes — we share necessary data with trusted third-party processors including cloud hosting providers like AWS and Google Cloud, payment processors like Stripe, and SMS and email delivery services. All are bound by strict Data Processing Agreements.

question: Does Ayonic share my data with authorities?
answer: We will disclose data to law enforcement, the Finanzamt (German tax office), or other authorities if legally required to do so.

question: Does Ayonic transfer data outside the EU?
answer: Our servers are primarily in the EU. Some third-party providers may be outside the EEA such as in the United States. We protect such transfers using Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission or the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.

question: Does Ayonic use cookies?
answer: Yes. Necessary cookies are used for platform operation, security, and basic functionality and do not require consent. Optional cookies for analytics, performance, personalization, and marketing are only used with your prior explicit consent under GDPR Art. 6(1)(a).

question: How do I manage or withdraw cookie consent?
answer: You can refuse or withdraw consent for optional cookies at any time via the cookie or consent management settings on the platform. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of prior processing and does not limit access to core platform features.

question: What is the legal agreement when using Ayonic?
answer: By using the platform you agree to the Ayonic Global Terms of Service, the Privacy Policy, the Ayonic Happiness Pledge where applicable, and any supplemental policies referenced therein. Together these form the Agreement.

question: Is Ayonic an employer of providers?
answer: No — Ayonic is not an employment agency and does not employ providers. Providers are independent professionals who run their own businesses, set their own rates, use their own tools, and have full discretion to accept or decline tasks. No employment, agency, or partnership relationship is created.

question: Who is a Client on Ayonic?
answer: A Client is any natural or legal person who requests or purchases services (Tasks) via the Platform.

question: Who is a Provider or Tasker on Ayonic?
answer: A Provider or Tasker is any independent professional or business entity offering and performing Tasks through the Platform.

question: What does verified or trusted mean on Ayonic?
answer: Labels such as verified or trusted indicate that a provider has completed certain steps on the platform. They are informational only and do not guarantee a provider's skills, qualifications, or reliability.

question: Who is responsible for choosing a provider?
answer: Clients are solely responsible for selecting providers and verifying that they have the necessary qualifications for the task.

question: What license do I get to use the Ayonic platform?
answer: Ayonic grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, and revocable license to access and use the platform where available, download the app on your personal device, and view content for personal use. Any rights not expressly granted are reserved.

question: Can I copy or reverse engineer Ayonic?
answer: No — you may not copy, download, redesign, reconfigure, reverse engineer, retransmit, or otherwise exploit any part of the platform without Ayonic's prior written consent.

question: What are my responsibilities as an Ayonic user?
answer: You must comply with all terms and applicable laws, meet minimum age requirements, act professionally, use your legal name, keep all task-related communications on the platform, and use only Ayonic-approved payment methods.

question: Can I use Ayonic for illegal purchases?
answer: No — you may not use the platform for the purchase or delivery of alcohol, controlled substances, or any illegal goods or services.

question: What are the requirements for providers on Ayonic?
answer: Providers must operate as an independent business entity, maintain an independent clientele, hold all required licenses and permits, maintain required insurance, perform tasks safely and professionally, and notify Ayonic of any relevant criminal convictions excluding minor traffic offenses.

question: Who owns the content on the Ayonic platform?
answer: All platform content including trademarks, text, software, designs, and other proprietary materials is owned by Ayonic or its licensors and protected by intellectual property laws. Users may not copy, modify, distribute, or exploit this content without prior written consent.

question: Can I use the Ayonic logo or brand name?
answer: No — the Ayonic service marks, logos, and trademarks may not be used by providers or other users without Ayonic's express prior written consent.

question: Is Ayonic responsible for third party websites?
answer: No — links to third-party websites on the platform are for reference and convenience only. Ayonic does not control or endorse those websites and is not responsible for their content. Your use of third-party websites is at your own risk.

question: How do I report a copyright infringement on Ayonic?
answer: Submit a written notice to Ayonic's designated Copyright Agent including a description of the infringement and its location on the platform, location of the original work, your contact information, a good-faith statement that the use is unauthorized, and your signature.

question: What warranties does Ayonic provide?
answer: The platform is provided with reasonable care and skill. Ayonic does not guarantee uninterrupted availability or error-free operation. For consumers, all statutory warranty rights under German and EU law remain fully unaffected.

question: What is Ayonic's liability for damages?
answer: Ayonic is fully liable for intent and gross negligence, injury to life, body, or health, and mandatory statutory provisions. For slight negligence, liability is limited to breach of cardinal obligations and foreseeable typical damages. Further liability is excluded to the extent permitted by law.

question: How are disputes resolved on Ayonic?
answer: Users are encouraged to first seek amicable resolution directly with Ayonic. For users in Germany and the EU, arbitration clauses do not apply. German courts have jurisdiction for German users. An EU Online Dispute Resolution platform is available at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

question: Which law governs the Ayonic agreement for German users?
answer: For users in Germany, the agreement is governed by the laws of the Federal Republic of Germany, excluding the CISG. Consumers may bring claims before courts of competent jurisdiction. For EU users, mandatory statutory provisions always prevail over any conflicting contractual terms.

question: Is an electronic signature valid on Ayonic?
answer: Yes — clicking a button or using any input device to indicate consent is treated as a legally valid electronic signature with the same legal effect as a handwritten signature. No third-party certification authority is required.

question: Can Ayonic assign or transfer the agreement?
answer: Yes — Ayonic may assign or transfer the agreement and will notify you. Where required by law you may choose to terminate the agreement. You may not assign or transfer the agreement without prior written approval from Ayonic.

question: What is the Ayonic Happiness Pledge?
answer: The Ayonic Happiness Pledge is a supplemental policy applicable in the country where a task or service is performed. It forms part of the overall agreement between the user and Ayonic along with the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

question: How do I delete my Ayonic account?
answer: Contact support@ayonic.com to request account deletion. Your profile data will be deleted within 30 days. Note that billing and transaction records may be retained for 6 to 10 years as required by German law.

question: Where is Ayonic based?
answer: Ayonic (Kyaani GmbH) is based at Leipziger Platz 15, 10117 Berlin, Germany. You can contact us at support@ayonic.com.

question: What is Ayonic's mission?
answer: Ayonic's mission is to empower users to take control of their time and talents. We connect clients with skilled professionals, champion fairness, clarity, and transparency, and make productivity simple, seamless, and safe.

question: Can Ayonic update its Terms of Service or Privacy Policy?
answer: Yes — Ayonic may update the Terms and Privacy Policy as features or laws change. Material changes will be communicated via email or in-app notification before they take effect.

question: What are Cardinal Obligations on Ayonic?
answer: Cardinal Obligations are essential contractual obligations whose fulfillment is necessary to achieve the purpose of the agreement and on whose compliance the user may reasonably rely. Ayonic is liable for breach of these even in cases of slight negligence.

question: Who are Consumers and Business Users on Ayonic?
answer: A Consumer (Verbraucher) is a natural person acting outside their trade or profession under §13 BGB. A Business User (Unternehmer) is a natural or legal person acting in a commercial or self-employed professional capacity under §14 BGB. Mandatory consumer rights always apply to consumers regardless of the agreement.
